title: corylus avellana var. macrochlamys
intro: Corylus avellana var. macrochlamys, also known as the English hazel or large-fruited hazel, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Corylus avellana var. macrochlamys include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as supporting immune function and digestion. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
